title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Namespace Identity
summary: The parser assumes RSS only, ignores Atom namespaces, treats optional fields as required, loses xml:base and language, and guesses extension meaning. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go namespace identity increment in standards-aware syndication vocabulary map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
